AST is usually elevated in hepatocellular damage, so that fits.
The levels here are 1230 and 900, which are definitely high.

The presence of HBs Ag (hepatitis B surface antigen) indicates that the liver is actively infected with the hepatitis B virus.
IgM anti-HBc (anti-hepatitis B core antibody) is also positive, which is an acute phase antibody that appears early in the infection.

Putting this all together, the patient has the classic presentation of acute hepatitis B.
